---
title: "3.4 Student and Community Constructors"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Student Class

Work in pairs for this challenge to create a `Student` class with constructors.

First, brainstorm in pairs to do the **Object-Oriented Design** for a `Student` class.

What data should we store about students?

Come up with at least four different instance variables and their data types.

## Student Requirements

### Constructor, print, and main

Write a `Student` class that has:

::: {.tight-list}
- four instance variables
- a constructor with four parameters to set all of the instance variables
- a `print()` method that uses `System.out.println` to print all instance variables
- a `main` method that constructs at least two `Student` objects using the constructor with different values
- calls to their `print()` methods
:::

## Active Code

### `activecode:: challenge-Student-class`

Create a class `Student` with four instance variables, a constructor, and a `print` method.

Write a `main` method that creates two `Student` objects with the constructor and calls their `print()` method.

## Student Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
/**
 * class Student with 4 instance variables, a constructor, a print method, and a
 * main method to test them.
 */
public class Student
{
    // Write 4 instance variables

    // Write a constructor with 4 parameters to initialize all of the instance
    // variables

    // Write a print method that prints all the instance variables
    public void print()
    {

    }

    // main method
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // Construct 2 Student objects using the constructor with different values

        // call their print() methods

    }
}
```
:::

## Student Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The Runestone tests check:

::: {.tight-list}
- two constructor calls using `= new Student(`
- one `public Student(` constructor
- two `.print()` calls
- four private instance variables
:::

## Design a Class for Your Community

### Continue from lesson 3.3

In the last lesson, you came up with a class of your own choice relevant to you or your community.

In this lesson, you will add a constructor to this class.

## Community Challenge Steps

### Constructor extension

::: {.tight-list}
- Consult your completed worksheet or your code from lesson 3.3 for the class name and three instance variables.
- Copy them into the active code exercise.
- Add a constructor with three parameters to set all of the instance variables.
- Write a `print()` method that uses `System.out.println` to print all instance variables.
- Write a `main` method that constructs at least two objects of your class using the constructor and then calls their `print()` methods.
:::

## Community Active Code

### `activecode:: community-challenge2`

Copy your class with its three instance variables from the previous community challenge.

Add a constructor with three parameters to set all of the instance variables to the given parameters.

Write a `print()` method and a `main` method that constructs at least two objects and calls their `print()` methods.

## Community Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class          // Add your class name here!
{
    // 1. copy in your instance variables for class from the last lesson

    // 2. Add a constructor with 3 parameters to set all of the instance variables to the given parameters.


    // 3. Write a print() method that uses System.out.println to print out all the instance variables.

    // 4. Write a main method that constructs at least 2 objects of your class
    // using the constructor and then calls their print() methods.
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
       // Construct 2 objects of your class to test the constructors


       // call the objects print() methods

    }
}
```
:::

## Community Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The Runestone tests check:

::: {.tight-list}
- at least three private instance variables
- a constructor with three parameters
- `print` output longer than 15 characters
- `main` output with at least three lines of text
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- design four Student instance variables before writing the constructor
- write one `Student` constructor with four parameters
- assign every constructor parameter into an instance variable
- construct two Student objects and call `print()` twice
- carry forward the community class name and three instance variables from Topic 3.3
- add a three-parameter constructor and print/main methods to the community class
:::

## End

### 3.4 complete

Next topic: 3.5 Methods: How to Write Them.
